line: GROVE_RESULT: success - implemented saw-core loop detection with 5-minute sliding window scoring and unit tests

line: - The current `saw-core` types had evolved beyond the earlier parser stubs, so `session.rs` needed alignment for `is_write`, sidechain flags, stderr/interrupted handling, and persisted-output parsing before tests would compile.

items: The current `saw-core` types had evolved beyond the earlier parser stubs, so `session.rs` needed alignment for `is_write`, sidechain flags, stderr/interrupted handling, and persisted-output parsing before tests would compile.

line: - The 5-minute sliding window is anchored to the latest observed tool call, so older writes correctly fall out of the rewrite count even when the same file appears earlier in history.

items: The 5-minute sliding window is anchored to the latest observed tool call, so older writes correctly fall out of the rewrite count even when the same file appears earlier in history.

line: - Used `ToolCall.is_write` plus known write-capable tool names as the rewrite signal, while still requiring a file path.

items: Used `ToolCall.is_write` plus known write-capable tool names as the rewrite signal, while still requiring a file path.

line: - Counted consecutive test failures by walking backward from the newest in-window call and stopping on the first non-failed-test call.

items: Counted consecutive test failures by walking backward from the newest in-window call and stopping on the first non-failed-test call.

line: - Treated test commands conservatively via common command patterns (`cargo test`, `pytest`, `go test`, `npm/pnpm/yarn/bun test`).

items: Treated test commands conservatively via common command patterns (`cargo test`, `pytest`, `go test`, `npm/pnpm/yarn/bun test`).
